source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they came to him and said: Hail, king of the Jews. And they gave him blows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Pilate therefore went forth again and saith to them: Behold, I bring him forth unto you, that you may know that I find no cause in him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: (Jesus therefore came forth, bearing the crown of thorns and the purple garment.) And he saith to them: Behold the Man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When the chief priests, therefore, and the servants had seen him, they cried out, saying: Crucify him, Crucify him. Pilate saith to them: Take him you, and crucify him: for I find no cause in him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Jews answered him: We have a law; and according to the law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When Pilate therefore had heard this saying, he feared the more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he entered into the hall again; and he said to Jesus: Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Pilate therefore saith to him: Speakest thou not to me? Knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and I have power to release thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus answered: Thou shouldst not have any power against me, unless it were given thee from above. Therefore, he that hath delivered me to thee hath the greater sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from henceforth Pilate sought to release him. But the Jews cried out, saying: If thou release this man, thou art not Caesar’s friend. For whosoever maketh himself a king speaketh against Caesar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now when Pilate had heard these words, he brought Jesus forth and sat down in the judgment seat, in the place that is called Lithostrotos, and in Hebrew Gabbatha.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it was the parasceve of the pasch, about the sixth hour: and he saith to the Jews: Behold your king.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they cried out: Away with him: Away with him: Crucify him. Pilate saith to them: shall I crucify your king? The chief priests answered: We have no king but Caesar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then therefore he delivered him to them to be crucified. And they took Jesus and led him forth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And bearing his own cross, he went forth to the place which is called Calvary, but in Hebrew Golgotha.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Where they crucified him, and with him two others, one on each side, and Jesus in the midst.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Pilate wrote a title also: and he put it upon the cross. And the writing was: JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This title therefore many of the Jews did read: because the place where Jesus was crucified was nigh to the city. And it was written in Hebrew, in Greek, and in Latin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate: Write not: The King of the Jews. But that he said: I am the King of the Jews.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Pilate answered: What I have written, I have written.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The soldiers therefore, when they had crucified him, took his garments, (and they made four parts, to every soldier a part) and also his coat. Now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They said then one to another: Let us not cut it but let us cast lots for it, whose it shall be; that the scripture might be fulfilled, saying: They have parted my garments among them, and upon my vesture they have cast lots. And the soldiers indeed did these things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now there stood by the cross of Jesus, his mother and his mother’s sister, Mary of Cleophas, and Mary Magdalen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When Jesus therefore had seen his mother and the disciple standing whom he loved, he saith to his mother: Woman, behold thy son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After that, he saith to the disciple: Behold thy mother. And from that hour, the disciple took her to his own.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Afterwards, Jesus knowing that all things were now accomplished, that the scripture might be fulfilled, said: I thirst.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now there was a vessel set there, full of vinegar. And they, putting a sponge full of vinegar about hyssop, put it to his mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus therefore, when he had taken the vinegar, said: It is consummated. And bowing his head, he gave up the ghost.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the Jews (because it was the parasceve), that the bodies might not remain upon the cross on the sabbath day (for that was a great sabbath day), besought Pilate that their legs might be broken: and that they might be taken away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The soldiers therefore came: and they broke the legs of the first, and of the other that was crucified with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But after they were come to Jesus, when they saw that he was already dead, they did not break his legs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But one of the soldiers with a spear opened his side: and immediately there came out blood and water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that saw it hath given testimony: and his testimony is true. And he knoweth that he saith true: that you also may believe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For these things were done that the scripture might be fulfilled: You shall not break a bone of him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again another scripture saith: They shall look on him whom they pierced.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after these things, Joseph of Arimathea (because he was a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews), besought Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus. And Pilate gave leave. He came therefore and took away the body of Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Nicodemus also came (he who at the first came to Jesus by night), bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They took therefore the body of Jesus and bound it in linen cloths, with the spices, as the manner of the Jews is to bury.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now there was in the place where he was crucified a garden: and in the garden a new sepulchre, wherein no man yet had been laid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There, therefore, because of the parasceve of the Jews, they laid Jesus: because the sepulchre was nigh at hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalen cometh early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre: and she saw the stone taken away from the sepulchre.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: She ran therefore and cometh to Simon Peter and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved and saith to them: They have taken away the Lord out of the sepulchre: and we know not where they have laid him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Peter therefore went out, and that other disciple: and they came to the sepulchre.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they both ran together: and that other disciple did outrun Peter and came first to the sepulchre.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he stooped down, he saw the linen cloths lying: but yet he went not in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then cometh Simon Peter, following him, and went into the sepulchre: and saw the linen cloths lying,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the napkin that had been about his head, not lying with the linen cloths, but apart, wrapped up into one place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then that other disciple also went in, who came first to the sepulchre: and he saw and believed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For as yet they knew not the scripture, that he must rise again from the dead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The disciples therefore departed again to their home.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Mary stood at the sepulchre without, weeping. Now as she was weeping, she stooped down and looked into the sepulchre,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she saw two angels in white, sitting, one at the head, and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had been laid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They say to her: Woman, why weepest thou? She saith to them: Because they have taken away my Lord: and I know not where they have laid him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When she had thus said, she turned herself back and saw Jesus standing: and she knew not that it was Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to her: Woman, why weepest thou? Whom seekest thou? She, thinking that it was the gardener, saith to him: Sir, if thou hast taken him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him: and I will take him away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to her: Mary. She turning, saith to him: Rabboni (which is to say, Master).

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to her: Do not touch me: for I am not yet ascended to my Father. But go to my brethren and say to them: I ascend to my Father and to your Father, to my God and to your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Mary Magdalen cometh and telleth the disciples: I have seen the Lord; and these things he said to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now when it was late the same day, the first of the week, and the doors were shut, where the disciples were gathered together, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the midst and said to them: Peace be to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had said this, he shewed them his hands and his side. The disciples therefore were glad, when they saw the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said therefore to them again: Peace be to you. As the Father hath sent me, I also send you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When he had said this, he breathed on them; and he said to them: Receive ye the Holy Ghost.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whose sins you shall forgive, they are forgiven them: and whose sins you shall retain, they are retained.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Thomas, one of the twelve, who is called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The other disciples therefore said to him: We have seen the Lord. But he said to them: Except I shall see in his hands the print of the nails and put my finger into the place of the nails and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after eight days, again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them. Jesus cometh, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst and said: Peace be to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he said to Thomas: Put in thy finger hither and see my hands. And bring hither thy hand and put it into my side. And be not faithless, but believing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thomas answered and said to him: My Lord and my God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to him: Because thou hast seen me, Thomas, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen and have believed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Many other signs also did Jesus in the sight of his disciples, which are not written in this book.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But these are written, that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God: and that believing, you may have life in his name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After this, Jesus shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias. And he shewed himself after this manner.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There were together: Simon Peter and Thomas, who is called Didymus, and Nathanael, who was of Cana of Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee and two others of his disciples.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Simon Peter saith to them: I go a fishing. They say to him: We also come with thee. And they went forth and entered into the ship: and that night they caught nothing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when the morning was come, Jesus stood on the shore: yet the disciples knew not that it was Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus therefore said to them: Children, have you any meat? They answered him: No.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He saith to them: Cast the net on the right side of the ship; and you shall find. They cast therefore: and now they were not able to draw it, for the multitude of fishes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved said to Peter: It is the Lord. Simon Peter, when he heard that it was the Lord, girt his coat about him (for he was naked) and cast himself into the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the other disciples came in the ship (for they were not far from the land, but as it were two hundred cubits) dragging the net with fishes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As soon then as they came to land they saw hot coals lying, and a fish laid thereon, and bread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to them: Bring hither of the fishes which you have now caught.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Simon Peter went up and drew the net to land, full of great fishes, one hundred and fifty-three. And although there were so many, the net was not broken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to them: Come and dine. And none of them who were at meat, durst ask him: Who art thou? Knowing that it was the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus cometh and taketh bread and giveth them: and fish in like manner.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is now the third time that Jesus was manifested to his disciples, after he was risen from the dead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When therefore they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter: Simon, son of John, lovest thou me more than these? He saith to him: Yea, Lord, thou knowest that I love thee. He saith to him: Feed my lambs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He saith to him again: Simon, son of John, lovest thou me? He saith to him: yea, Lord, thou knowest that I love thee. He saith to him: Feed my lambs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to him the third time: Simon, son of John, lovest thou me? Peter was grieved because he had said to him the third time: Lovest thou me? And he said to him: Lord, thou knowest all things: thou knowest that I love thee. He said to him: Feed my sheep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen, amen, I say to thee, When thou wast younger, thou didst gird thyself and didst walk where thou wouldst. But when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee and lead thee whither thou wouldst not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this he said, signifying by what death he should glorify God. And when he had said this, he saith to him: Follow me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Peter turning about, saw that disciple whom Jesus loved following, who also leaned on his breast at supper and said: Lord, who is he that shall betray thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Him therefore when Peter had seen, he saith to Jesus: Lord, and what shall this man do?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to him: So I will have him to remain till I come, what is it to thee? Follow thou me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This saying therefore went abroad among the brethren, that that disciple should not die. And Jesus did not say to him: He should not die; but: So I will have him to remain till I come, what is it to thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is that disciple who giveth testimony of these things and hath written these things: and we know that his testimony is true.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But there are also many other things which Jesus did which, if they were written every one, the world itself. I think, would not be able to contain the books that should be written.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
